# Quality Checklist Template

## Overview
The Quality Checklist ensures that project deliverables meet defined standards and acceptance
criteria. It supports systematic quality control throughout the lifecycle and reduces the
risk of defects reaching stakeholders.

**Purpose:** Standardize QA processes and ensure consistent deliverable quality.  
**When to use:** Planning, execution, testing, and acceptance activities.  
**Who uses it:** Project Manager, Quality Assurance, Team Members, Stakeholders.

---

### Project Quality Information
| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Project name |  |
| Quality manager |  |
| Quality standards |  |

| Objective | Target | Current | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Defect rate |  |  |  |
| Customer satisfaction |  |  |  |
| First-time-right |  |  |  |

---

## Universal Quality Checklist
### Planning Phase Quality
- [ ] **Quality management plan created**
  - Standards defined
  - Metrics identified
  - Roles assigned
  - Processes documented
- [ ] **Requirements quality review**
  - Clear and unambiguous
  - Testable and measurable
  - Traceability established
  - Stakeholder sign-off obtained
- [ ] **Design quality standards**
  - Design standards documented
  - Review process defined
  - Templates created
  - Approval criteria established

### Execution Phase Quality
- [ ] **Work product quality control**
  - Quality checks performed at intervals
  - Peer reviews conducted
  - Metrics tracked
  - Non-conformances addressed
- [ ] **Process quality assurance**
  - Processes followed as defined
  - Deviations documented
  - Improvements identified
  - Audits conducted
- [ ] **Team quality training**
  - Quality standards trained
  - Tools and techniques taught
  - Responsibilities communicated
  - Quality culture promoted

### Monitoring Phase Quality
- [ ] **Quality metrics tracking**
  - KPIs monitored regularly
  - Trends analyzed
  - Corrective actions taken
  - Reports generated
- [ ] **Continuous improvement**
  - Lessons learned captured
  - Improvements implemented
  - Best practices shared
  - Feedback incorporated

---

## Deliverable-Specific Quality Checklists
### Document Quality Checklist
#### Content Quality
- [ ] Accuracy and completeness
- [ ] Clarity and readability
- [ ] Consistent terminology and formatting

#### Format and Presentation
- [ ] Standard template used
- [ ] Proper headings and structure
- [ ] Visual elements clear and relevant
- [ ] Spelling/grammar checked

### Software Quality Checklist
#### Functional Quality
- [ ] Requirements compliance
- [ ] Usability standards met
- [ ] Performance standards met
- [ ] Integration points validated

#### Technical Quality
- [ ] Coding standards followed
- [ ] Unit/integration tests executed
- [ ] Security practices applied
- [ ] Documentation complete

### Process Quality Checklist
#### Process Design
- [ ] Process steps documented
- [ ] Roles and responsibilities identified
- [ ] Inputs/outputs specified
- [ ] Exceptions identified

#### Process Implementation
- [ ] Process communicated to users
- [ ] Training delivered
- [ ] Monitoring established
- [ ] Support model in place

### Training Quality Checklist
#### Training Content
- [ ] Learning objectives defined
- [ ] Content accurate and current
- [ ] Materials comprehensive
- [ ] Accessibility checked

#### Training Delivery
- [ ] Trainers prepared
- [ ] Environment ready
- [ ] Materials distributed
- [ ] Feedback collected

---

## Quality Review Processes
### Peer Review Process
- [ ] Review objectives defined
- [ ] Reviewers selected
- [ ] Review materials distributed
- [ ] Findings documented and prioritized
- [ ] Actions assigned and verified

### Testing and Validation
- [ ] Test strategy defined
- [ ] Test cases prepared
- [ ] Test execution recorded
- [ ] Defects logged and tracked
- [ ] Test summary reported

### Acceptance Process
- [ ] Acceptance criteria defined
- [ ] Acceptance tests prepared
- [ ] Stakeholders identified
- [ ] Sign-off captured

---

## Quality Tools and Techniques
### Quality Planning Tools
- [ ] Quality Function Deployment (QFD)
- [ ] Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA)
- [ ] Design of Experiments (DOE)

### Quality Control Tools
- [ ] Statistical Process Control (SPC)
- [ ] Pareto analysis
- [ ] Root cause analysis

### Quality Improvement Tools
- [ ] Plan-Do-Check-Act (PDCA)
- [ ] Six Sigma tools
- [ ] Lean quality principles

---

## Quality Risk Management
- [ ] Quality risks identified and assessed
- [ ] Mitigation strategies implemented
- [ ] Contingency plans documented
- [ ] Risks reviewed and updated

---

## Continuous Quality Improvement
### Feedback Loops
- [ ] Customer feedback captured and analyzed
- [ ] Internal feedback captured and analyzed
- [ ] Lessons learned documented and shared

### Quality Culture
- [ ] Quality awareness communicated
- [ ] Quality achievements recognized
- [ ] Training and development provided

---

## Customization Guidelines
### Agile Projects
- [ ] Definition of Done criteria
- [ ] Sprint review quality gates
- [ ] Continuous integration quality checks

### Traditional Projects
- [ ] Phase-gate quality reviews
- [ ] Deliverable sign-offs obtained
- [ ] Quality baseline established

### Hybrid Projects
- [ ] Risk-based quality planning
- [ ] Adaptive quality controls
- [ ] Integrated governance

checklist to ensure your project delivers excellence.*
